title: Lachemilla mandoniana
intro: Lachemilla mandoniana, also known as Chilean rock jasmine, is a flowering plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Lachemilla mandoniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Lachemilla mandoniana is used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Lachemilla mandoniana is classified within the family Rosaceae. This family includes a wide variety of plants commonly known as roses or rose-like species. Lachemilla mandoniana is a member of the genus Lachemilla, which consists of various small, delicate plants. The genus Lachemilla is further divided into multiple species, with Lachemilla mandoniana being one of them. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Lachemilla mandoniana shares characteristics with other plants in this family.

benefits: Lachemilla mandoniana, also known as rock pink or Chilean rock pink, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. These constituents include iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
